blocks:
Surah Al-Qadr

Meccan, and it is said Medinan. Its number of verses is six verses according to the Meccan and Syrian [reciters], and five according to the rest. The point of difference is the third verse لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ according to the Meccan and Syrian [reciters]. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١)** إِنَّا أَنْزَلْناهُ فِي لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ ** (1) Indeed We sent it down on the Night of Ordainment. meaning the Quran.

(٢) وَما أَدْراكَ ما لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ ** (2) And what will show you what the Night of Ordainment is? In it is an exaltation for it, and it was only named the Night of al-Qadr because in it every matter is decreed (يُقَدَّر) that will occur in that year until the like of it in the following [year].

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to me: "O Ali, do you know the meaning of لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ?" So I said: "No, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "Indeed, Allah, the Almighty, decreed (قَدَّرَ) in it what is to be until the Day of Resurrection. And among what He decreed was your wilayah (وِلايَتُكَ) and the wilayah (وِلايَة) of the Imams عليهم السلام from your progeny until the Day of Resurrection." And the meaning of the descent of the Quran therein has already passed in the Ninth Introduction (المقدّمة التاسعة) of this book.

(٣)لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ خَيْرٌ مِنْ أَلْفِ شَهْرٍ. (3) The Night of Ordainment is better than a thousand months.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was shown in his sleep that Banu Umayyah would ascend his pulpit after him and mislead the people backward (على الصراط القهقرى) from the path. He awoke distressed and sorrowful. He said: Then Gabriel descended upon him and said: "O Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, why do I see you distressed and sorrowful?" He said: "O Gabriel, I saw Banu Umayyah this night ascending my pulpit after me, misleading the people backward from the path." Gabriel said: "By the One Who sent you with the truth as a Prophet, I was not informed of this." Then he ascended to the heaven, and it was not long before he descended upon him with verses from the Quran to comfort him. He (Gabriel) said: أَفَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ مَتَّعْنَاهُمْ سِنِينَ 2 ثُمَّ جَاءَهُمْ مَا كَانُوا يُوعَدُونَ 3 مَا أَغْنَى عَنْهُمْ مَا كَانُوا يُمَتَّعُونَ 4 (Do you see, if We let them enjoy for some years, 2 then there comes to them what they were promised, 3 what they were enjoying will not avail them?) And He revealed to him: إِنَّا أَنْزَلْنَاهُ فِي لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ 1 وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ 2 لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ خَيْرٌ مِنْ أَلْفِ شَهْرٍ 3 (Indeed, We sent it down on the Night of Ordainment. 1 What will convey to you what the Night of Ordainment is? 2 The Night of Ordainment is better than a thousand months.) Allah made the Night of Ordainment for His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم better than the thousand months of the reign of Banu Umayyah.

And there are other narrations with this meaning, both in this context and elsewhere. And al-Qummi said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was shown as if monkeys were ascending his pulpit, and this grieved him. So Allah revealed the Surah of al-Qadr: إِنَّا أَنْزَلْنَاهُ فِي لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ 1 وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ 2 لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ خَيْرٌ مِنْ أَلْفِ شَهْرٍ 3 (Indeed, We sent it down on the Night of Ordainment. 1 What will convey to you what the Night of Ordainment is? 2 The Night of Ordainment is better than a thousand months.) which is the reign of Banu Umayyah, in which there is no Night of Ordainment.

In al-Majma', narrated from Ibn Abbas, he said: A man from the Children of Israel was mentioned to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم who had carried weapons on his shoulder in the way of Allah for a thousand months. He was greatly astonished by this and wished that such a thing could be for his Ummah. So he said: "O Lord, You have made my Ummah the shortest-lived of nations and the least in deeds." So Allah granted him the Night of Ordainment and said: لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ خَيْرٌ مِنْ أَلْفِ شَهْرٍ 3 (The Night of Ordainment is better than a thousand months.) which is the period the Israelite carried weapons in the way of Allah, [granted] to you and your Ummah after you until the Day of Resurrection, in every Ramadan.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Almighty's saying: إِنَّا أَنْزَلْنَاهُ فِي لَيْلَةٍ مُبَارَكَةٍ 3 (Indeed, We sent it down on a blessed night). He said: "Yes, لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ (the Night of Ordainment). And it is in every year during the month of Ramadan, in the last ten [nights]. The Quran was only revealed on the Night of Ordainment."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ (the Night of Ordainment). He said: "Seek it on the night of the twenty-first or the night of the twenty-third." And in a narration: "The night of the nineteenth, the twenty-first, and the twenty-third." It was asked: "If a person is overcome by weakness (الفترة) or illness (علّة), which of these should he rely upon?" He replied: "The twenty-third."

And narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both): Its sign (علامتها) is that its scent is pleasant, and if it is in cold weather, it becomes warm, and if it is in hot weather, it becomes cool.

And in a narration of the general public (العامّة): It is neither hot nor cold, and the sun rises on its morning without rays (شعاع).

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Deeds performed therein are better than deeds performed in a thousand months in which there is no Night of Ordainment.

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked: "Do you know لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ (the Night of Ordainment)?" He replied: "And how could we not know, when the angels circulate around us during it?"

(٤) تَنَزَّلُ الْمَلائِكَةُ وَالرُّوحُ فِيها بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِمْ مِنْ كُلِّ أَمْرٍ (4) In it the angels and the Spirit descend by the leave of their Lord, with every command.

Al-Qummi said: The angels and the Holy Spirit (روح القدس) descend upon the Imam of the Age (امام زمان) and hand over to him what they have written.

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When it is لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ ** (the Night of Ordainment), the angels, the Spirit (الرّوح), and the scribes (الكتبة) descend to the lowest heaven (السماء الدنيا) and they write down what will occur of Allah's decree (قضاء الله) in that year. The narration [continues, but is summarized here]. It has already passed in Surat al-Ra'd, and in al-Kafi: what is similar in meaning.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Verily, الرُّوحُ ** (the Spirit) is greater than Gabriel (جبرئيل), and Gabriel (جبرئيل) is greater than الْمَلائِكَةُ ** (the angels), and that He (الرُّوح) is a creation greater than the angels. Does not Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, say: تَنَزَّلُ الْمَلائِكَةُ وَالرُّوحُ. (The angels and the Spirit descend.)

(٥) سَلامٌ هِيَ حَتَّى مَطْلَعِ الْفَجْرِ (5) Peace it is until the rise of dawn. Al-Qummi said: It is a greeting (تحيّة) with which the Imam is greeted until the dawn rises. And in Al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): He says: "My angels and My Spirit (روحي) convey My peace (سلامي) upon you, O Muhammad, from the moment they descend until the rise of dawn." And in his supplication (دعائه) for the entry of the month of Ramadan: "A peace (سلام) whose blessing is continuous until the rising of dawn, upon whomever He wills among His servants, by virtue of what He has decreed of His judgment (قضائه)." And maṭliʿ (مطلع) was read with a kasrah on the lām. In Thawab al-A'mal and Al-Majma', narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Whoever recites إِنَّا أَنزَلْنَاهُ فِي لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ (Indeed, We sent it down on the Night of Ordainment) and raises his voice with it, is like one who draws his sword in the way of Allah. And whoever recites it secretly is like one who is drenched in his own blood (المتشحّط بدمه) in the way of Allah. And whoever recites it secretly many times, Allah erases a thousand sins from his sins.
